assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_016",
  "rule_id": "Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Enforce for root User",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "rationale": "The setting controls password complexity for root via pam_pwquality and can be safely enabled without risking lockout when applied correctly.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Potential lockout if root password does not meet complexity requirements"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Check existing enforce_for_root value in /etc/security/pwquality.conf",
    "Apply the provided remediation script",
    "Verify that root password complies with complexity rules",
    "Test root login after change"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": true,
  "touches_networking": false,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}

But still safe. We'll list ["Potential lockout if root password does not meet complexity"].

safe_next_steps: array of strings: maybe ["Verify current enforce_for_root setting", "Apply remediation script", "Validate password complexity requirements", "Test root login after change"].
